I think having the same skill under my belt helps. Besides, look at the situation here. It's easy to guess how you'd act, and I'd also guess Federico's guessed.
There's no winning with you.
Plan A should've been dragging him off for drinks, after all.
Followed by him carting you back to the Lateran?
This monastery's guests have more swagger than you think. Can't say Federico would care to wrangle me once it's all going down.
Sounds like trouble really is brewing here.
So, Oren, what are you and Serpilia planning exactly?
Once you hear me out, are you gonna help me?
Thaaat... remains to be seen.
I'd have to hear the answer first to decide.
Federico, I've got bad news.
Uh... wait, is there anyone with you?
Richele is not here.
Who cares about him!
I mean... is that Fortuna kid with you?
No. Richele escorted her to her accommodations.
If you have discovered anything, please inform me. I require confirmation of the aggrieved citizen's condition.
Fine. Then I'll give it to you straight—we were too late.
Confirmed deceased?
Confirmed. It was a fatal gunshot wound. Barring a true miracle, she's not coming back to life.
Oh, and the guy with me called Clément. He said he was going to pick some flowers, and I didn't care. I let him go.
He was out of his mind when he left...
Flowers?
Per the intel. The abbey's specialty. Not many left right now, I gather, and that's not important.
The whole was an accident, Federico. The girl wasn't lying. Accidental discharge. It might not... be all that severe...
His Holiness will judge.
Sure... I mean, even Mostima flies with him. I'm sure Our Holiness will forgive with wide arms just like always.
I'll contact Richele in a bit—I'm sure he misses being free range. It'll be me watching the girl. I won't let her run off.
As for how Lemuen and Oren are doing...
Has Oren Argiolas made contact with you?
Am I under suspicion here?
By my judgment, yes.
Oren's movements were not placed under any restrictions. Failure to contact the Lateran is conduct of his own subjective.
The Sarkaz are involved in these affairs. I conjecture Oren would not immediately leave. If he wanted all leeway for operation, it would require finding a collaborator.
And so you suspect me... But what about Richele? No thoughts about him?
Richele does not have as ample time to act alone as you do. His level of suspicion ranks below yours.
Hahaha. You're a smart cookie, Federico.
Here's my comment: no comment. All guesses here are yours. I haven't said a thing.
Good? Good. I'll be in touch.
Oh, yeah, is the Abbot there?
Yes.
Uh... are you going to relay the news to him?
That is unnecessary.
He has heard it.
...Delfina...
I must see for myself. I need to... see the child...
Your Excellency may act freely.
However, Serpilia would not misjudge. I do not recommend spending additional time on reconfirming.
I am tentatively assuming Oren Argiolas is still aboard, and has some sort of scheme.
I must now ascertain in detail all movements he made prior to his disappearance staying at this monastery, as well as all recent absences and returns of monastery personnel. I ask you comply.
Reverend Executor, what is necessity to you? What line do you draw between the necessary and unnecessary?
Can you truly not hear your kin lamenting? Does emotion truly mean nothing in your mind?
I will not deny that.
And should that be, you are no worthy harbinger of Paradise.
And should you represent Laterano...
Laterano is no worthy shepherd of my faith.
Please answer my questions, Your Excellency: Has Oren acted abnormally in any way within the monastery? Have any other people lodged recently at the monastery?
My words are wasted on you.
Do as you will, Reverend Executor.
Finished...
Trust him to use up every last page of a notebook this thick.
Lemuen heaves a sigh, and closes the antique plain colored notebook shut.
Her fingers trace over the nicks and scrapes that decorate the cover, each one hiding a story of its own.
If back then...
Oh, what am I thinking?
Right, now then... Hm, it's time I got to work myself.
With Serpilia's group already here, I can't keep on dawdling.
Hm?
Is someone outside? If you're looking for me, you can come right in.
Well, well, that's not a guest I'd expect.
Sorry, visitor, but you came at a bad time—I don't have any tea to offer you.
We don't usually greet our visitors at gunpoint, save for special occasions.
Pardon me if you're dissatisfied with your treatment in any way.
(Indecipherable howling)
(Not reacting at all to what I say. Is communication completely out the window?)
(Not many human features left to show. Is it all hidden under those scales?)
(...I remember Iberian files mentioning this sort of mutation before...)
You couldn't be...
Oofff...
Nope, the wheelchair's not going to cut it.
(Strange. Something's not right.)
(Is its target... not me?)
(Where is it aiming? What is it trying to—)
Too close...!
(Forget it, I'll have to worry about that later!)
Nuh-uh, just because I'm physically handicapped doesn't mean you can pick on me, visitor!
(Angry howling)
Stop right there—!
Nope—I can't just let you slip away!
When the elderly chat, the topic inevitably turns to Laterano
They call it bright, abundant, safe for all, disturbed by none.
They say it's true paradise on Terra.
The truth is, they all know the monastery's been adrift for decades, and only a dwindling few remain who've actually seen Laterano before. Perhaps two or three, or maybe just it's Bishop Stefano now.
They bring up Laterano, but the object of their nostalgia is a dream they're long overdue to wake from.
A dream where the sun shines and the flowers are underfoot forever.
Just as the monastery used to be, all those years ago.
They're about to wither.
They bloomed earlier than last year. I could guess they'd wither sooner too. I just didn't think it'd be this soon...
The few you picked were in wonderful blossom. They're not about to wilt yet.
How pretty. Who'll be receiving these?
I'm too late. There's no giving them now.
They can't save anyone...
Sorrow. Pain. Despair.
Mm... such complex overtones. There are other emotions hidden inside you, too...
Are you angered?
I wouldn't dare... You're a guest of His Excellency, Seniora Arturia.
You wouldn't dare, that's all. A very honest answer.
My intuition has always been unusually accurate, Senior Florist. You seem to loath me a little.
No, not at all. You needn't worry.
If anything is troubling you, I could be of help to you, I think.
Help...? Er, I'm not sure what you mean, Seniora Arturia.
What would you help with?
This hinges on your thoughts. It's not something for me to decide.
Of course, you may not need any help at all. Call me meddlesome if you wish, or a bearer of bad intent, none of which I would mind. It's for you to say.
I just want to hear your thoughts... your own thoughts.
You're a strange one, Seniora Arturia.
You flatter me.
You say you can help me...
Of course, so long as you need.
No, there's no need.
Perhaps you could... well, there's a great many you can help, but you can't help me.
Oh?
Seniora Arturia, would you claim to be able to raise the dead?
No.
Can you make the harvest rich, or see that the cold winds stop?
I'm very sorry, but I can't.
Then can you return us to your original lives, see that the barriers of old are no more?
You could never.
Then what of you, Clément?
Have you never once entertained being able to solve all you speak of, by your own hands?
...I've tried...
But it was no use. I've tried more than once, but I couldn't do it.
The more effort we put in, the worse things get, more often than not...
No one can make it all better. The Abbot can't, Gerald can't... and neither can I.
You can't help me, Seniora Arturia.
Please... don't bring this up again. And don't think I need the help of someone like you.
You ask what my thoughts are. Let me tell you: I have no thoughts at all.
What else can I come up with? The coming winter is too cold.
Cold. Yes, this heart is like soil frozen through, beneath a sheet of iciness...
Yet I can feel notes lurking beneath the ice... I wonder how powerfully they will move us?
It's not yet time. This composition is not bound for completion yet...
Such unbearableness, such tolerance... mm... in times like these, I cannot help myself.
Let me see...
Ah, this melody is a perfect fit for the occasion.
Federico.
I told you, your melodies are too rhythmic, too unperturbed. You should know that an all-too-rational melody will lack the sentiments to shake people. There's no warmth, nor passion...
Sometimes, I can only be curious.
Federico, when will you finally realize... why it is you search?
(Strings?)
(No, this is more than strings. There are Originium Arts effecting something within.)
(These Arts, and this melody. Could they be...?)
(The sounds are nearing.)
(Are they above?)
What's today's song? It's beautiful.
Do you hear music every day here?
Erk! Wh—Who...?
I am Executor Federico of the Notarial Hall. Please answer my question.
Notarial Hall? What does that even...? Oh, I see, you're that guest from Laterano they were talking about, aren't you?
Sometimes you can, er, hear this music around this time? I'm not too sure myself.
Oh, right! I believe I heard about someone by the sacrarium who plays a cello or something...
Where is the sacrarium?
On the roof. Take the stairs outside and you'll be right—wait, ex-excuse me, Senior?!
What was the rush...? Such a strange man.
Welcome back, boss.
Yeah. How ready are we to move out?
More or less good. We don't have much to take with us, and we'd be overburdened anyway if we did.
But there's been no word from the young ones we sent to scout the way... They might not make it in time for tomorrow morning.
Wait a little longer.
Stefano and I have talked it over. That we're leaving... he'll let it be. So no need to rush it now. If we still hear nothing by nightfall, then I'll take a team myself to find them.
Sure. I'll update everyone in a minute.
Oh, boss! There's one other thing...
What is it?
Hyman hasn't come back either... I looked around for her, but she's nowhere to be found.
Is Raimund still gone as well?
Yes.
Got it. Leave this to me.
Okay.
But if Hyman never shows up, should we... wait?
Tomorrow morning at the latest. If anyone's still not back after Matins, you and Raimund will set off with rest as planned.
But...
I'll stay behind. Dead or alive, I'll find Hyman.
I swear, I'd never abandon a single one of you.
That's all we're worried about.
*mumble*, you always take too much on yourself.
Let's not bring up that name again.
You call me boss. I have a duty to find you a way out.
And I see you walking down a dead end.
Hahaha. I know what I'm doing.
Forget all that, no need to look so sad. Go on, get everything good and ready.
Shift change.
Wargh...!
There's a fright... hi there, Serpilia.
Who else would you expect? Does Federico ever bother with this sort of work?
How's the girl in there...? Doing bad?
Bad in a way. She hasn't said a word. Seems she's in shock.
I eyed over the scene. Aren't you the least bit curious about what actually happened?
I... can more or less guess.
You and I both know how powerful a gun is. With that much blood loss... anyone would be doomed, wouldn't they?
Your face says I'm on the mark.
Did you tell her?
What? Oh, as in, that she might already be dead? Of course not.
She's unsettled right now. Telling her the truth could just as well cause more problems.
So what is your plan? Lie to her and say nothing's wrong, muddle through, figure out the rest later, I bet?
You make it sound so bad... but still, that's basically it.
Serpilia, something seems a little fishy with your emotions. What happened?
None of your business. I'm doing great.
If you say so, then. No problems in Serpilia-land.
Okay. I'll leave her to you.
Hmph. Every undependable bastard's on parade today.
(An accident caused by an unintentional discharge...)
(Ha. And so what?)
(If I hadn't helped her...)
What? Make it quick.
Wait, what do you mean Sarkaz in the outer vicinity? Are they still trying to break your blockade?
Just a few? Scouting the road?
Fine, got it. What about Oren?
No, you can leave Oren to me. I'll contact him.
Listen, here's what you guys do next...
...Seriously... what a good runner!
*huff*...
Or maybe I just lost it...
(Back in the old days, I would've put them away clean in a fraction of the time.)
(Times like these... I can't help but feel a little mad.)
There you are...!
Guess it's not the time to brood just yet.
(The sound of strings is growing clear. I am steadily approaching the source.)
(But the Arts are diminishing in strength. Are they giving up casting, or... hm?)
Who is it?
Senior Federico? What are you doing here...?
I was not referring to you.
Er, excuse me...?
Are you lost? If you don't mind, I can—
Stop talking.
Mmff?! Mrmmffff!!
What... What was that? That monster...
I do not know.
It's fled downstairs! Not good... Gerald's group live down there!
Stay still.
Someone is here.
I heard gunfire this way, so I came to test my luck.
Cardinal Aide Lemuen, Serpilia's report stated that you would not be operating in the field.
Don't blame her. If I'm honest, I didn't think I would be either.
But then came a slight twist...
We'll catch up later. Are you on your own, Federico? Is nobody else operating with you?
I am on my own.
Then let's keep it short. I'm on the trail of a surprise... visitor.
I won't keep you too long...
So here, be good and tell me.
Which way did my 'visitor' happen to go?

eps.name: My Life I Gave for You

lines.x: I'm a little surprised.
Because I've come to turn my back on the faith?
Because of what you're evading, Stefano.
I don't know whether you're under some outside influence, but if...
There are no ifs, Aulus.
I am not so far gone that Arts would sway me.
There are times when Arts doesn't need to change our minds at all. All too many things can impact us, Stefano.
You are asking that I reconsider. Why, heretic priest?
Is this decision of mine not what you always wanted to see?
I can't deny it.
But in turn, I have to recognize that back when this extraordinary building was still in Iberia, it was my fortune to study here, and those days were precious like little else to me then.
In this way, Stefano, I'm just like you. I feel for this place, just as you do.
I still remember the cleric who came with you from Laterano.
I remember very many.
The one you recall took his gun, sixty years ago, and made the decision to fall in line with the Church of then. While I...
I chose to stay behind, and protect those who were left.
And this fed your faith, enduring until now.
If you know so well, why would you still have me continue to wait, to endure?
Are you about to refute all that you've maintained and proclaimed, just to turn around and tell me to deliberate, exhort me to stay fast?
Of course not. Stefano, you know I wouldn't.
But at core, I walk the path I believe is right, and so I would hope I could say the same of you.
Tomorrow morning, should you still be committed, I will consider it your choice then.
I will prepare what you need.
If you are not yet decided, I will advise you to continue contemplation.
Whatever it may be... I hope your decision will be that you find truest.
Aulus, you wrongly assume one thing.
And what is right?
I am lost for any other choice.
Very well. Should this be Truth's ordain...
Then I will welcome you, Stefano, my brother anew.
Mind if I come in?
...You can.
'Scuse me.
You're not doing too hot, then?
...Can I ask a question?
Sure.
Is... Is Fina not...?
With us anymore?
Not to push you, but I'd prefer some other question.
I'm not really up for lying to you.
Okay.
Actually, I had an inkling all along...
When it happened, I just suddenly—couldn't feel Fina anymore... even though she was so angry, and so upset...
And she was right there in front of me, right...? But I couldn't find her anywhere...
If I hadn't helped you fix up that gun...
No!
Don't say it, please...
...Alright.
I felt the horns—on my head. They're horns just like Raimund's.
I can't ever pray holding my gun again, but at least...
I finally—fit in, with Raimund, and Caroline...
I fit in with everybody.
Hah, the flowers weren't blooming this early last year. Funny timing now, hm?
No complaints, though. One last bloom for the road.
And so...
That'll do it...
Repeat that... we have a fallen angel? That Fortuna girl?
Yes.
I wasn't expecting that one, let me tell you...
Fallen... Our only option is to hold on until we get back, then let His Holiness decide. It's not something we can handle right here.

But that surprise visitor who barged into my room is a different story. Federico, there's some things I'll explain to you later... for now, anything's a non-starter unless we find that visitor, whatever it is.
I require your assistance, Executor Federico.
It has mobility on its side. I managed to chase it down this far, but that was a stretch, and I've lost it now anyway.
Huh, no immediate reply?
That's rare. You're making me feel like something bad happened to you... Hey, I see those knit brows. Something bad did happen, then?
...I heard strings.
Strings? Did... you? I don't think I heard anything...?
Based on my inference, the sound came from the sacrarium on the top floor.
Right...
Let me be real. I've gotten to know the length and breadth of your work at the Notarial Hall, which is why I'm relatively willing to trust your judgment.
I am now formally querying you—
Notarial Hall Executor Federico, do you truly believe this unidentified, unascertained, and possibly completely benign sound of strings—
Stands to be more important than the request for assistance I'm making of you?
It does not.
Obviously not.
The occurrence of the sound is a chance event. I have suspicions about it, but I cannot deny that I as of yet find no direct correlation between it and our catalog of current affairs.
Based on present circumstances, I judge that priority should be given to providing requisite assistance, in tandem operation with you.
Heheh. All done then?
Alright, alright, I'm not here to bully you anyway. Just, I do need to check in with how you're doing real quick.
I see now.
Point me in a direction. I'll continue target pursuit, and I'll leave you to sort out the strings debacle.
This mission requires that we prioritize assuring yours and Oren's safety.
Due diligence is great and all, but I say priority's the kind of thing you need to judge yourself.
I can't sense your emotions, Federico. Still, I have this Liberi friend, see, who's the reason I learned long ago how to guess what people are thinking. Couldn't do empathy with her, after all.
She might be a lot easier to figure out than you are, but some things carry over.
One teensy little word of advice—maybe sometimes, you need to learn to trust your instinct.
That settles that. We don't have time to waste... and I've gotta force myself to be a little active anyway.
Operation a-go! Move out, everyone~
Speed up.
Senior Federico! W—Wait one moment, please!
What exactly do... you think is up there? It was just the sound of strings, and we can't even hear it anymore!
Are—Are we really going to let the seniora brave the danger on her own? She's in a wheelchair, after all...
You have no need to worry.
How could I not worry? I just saw it myself... it was a monster!
Lemuen was admitted into the Lateran by personal invitation of a Cardinal of the Seventh Tribunal. Her strength defies any contempt.
Do not underestimate her.
I don't quite follow what you're telling me—I assume you mean she's just as mighty herself?
But... what if?
Speed up. Or stay where you are and wait.
I suggest you pick the latter. It is the safer option.
(Empty?)
(No.)
Subtle noises spill through the spacious sacrarium. The loading of the gun keeps audible lockstep rhythm.
The executor suddenly points.
Come out.
I will only warn you once more. Come out from there.
Ah... he found us.
He found us. He's just like the lady said...
Yeah, the big man found us right away!
Is—Is he really the man she was talking about?
He's got the wings and ring and they're all black. It's him, must be.
Mister, my name is Erendel!
I'm Estara...
Where is the woman who described me to you?
He looks really, really scary...
You're too scary! And you didn't even say your name... that's just rude!
Mamma says if you don't know someone's name and they're a stranger, you shouldn't tell them anything...
Federico.
Senior Federico's scary. Is he a good guy...?
I bet he is! Just like in the story.
W—We can tell him, then, right?
Probably we can?
Um... well...
Someone told us...
We had to wait here for a really scary big man with a black ring on his head just like she has!
And she—she said you'd find Mamma with us...
Yeah! And also she said we were only allowed to wait a little, tiny bit! And if you didn't come, then we come out ourselves.
Um... she said we couldn't stay here forever.
And also, also, she said...
Eek!
Senior, Senior! Quick... there's a fire!
Wah!
Waargh!
Get out for now.
Cover your mouth and nose. A fire has broken out.

eps.name: My Life I Gave for You

lines.x: Fire devours all indiscriminately.
One fire can destroy everything, no matter how precious, no matter how treasured.
Gerald once said the Sarkaz have no home, only war that follows them wherever they go. They swore fealty to their lord in the hope of finding a home of their own.
He left because he became disappointed.
I did not dare to ask why he was disappointed.
We were full of hope when we first came here.
We were happy despite the hardship.
But now...
Now, they are leaving. Is it because life here has disappointed him in the same way?
What about me?
I am watching the things I treasured burn before my eyes.
Burning.
Fading away.
Gone... all gone...
Fire! Fire!
The sacrarium is burning! Get water! Water!
What's going on? Is that a fire?
Raimund! What are you doing here? I thought you had...
Now's not the time for that!
Y-You're right!
The sacrarium's on fire! Give me a hand!
The sacrarium?!
How did the sacrarium catch fire?
How should I know?! Fires have happened before, but nothing as bad as this one. And the timing!
We need to put it out! Thank goodness it wasn't the residences...
Keep fighting the fire. I'll get help.
Water! *cough* More water!
The fire's too fierce!
Careful of the smoke! Close the door, that might keep the fire from spreading...
What are you talking about?!
It's the sacrarium! The holy statue is in there! Closing the door won't put the fire out!
That's not what I mean! But it's too dangerous right now!
A wise decision.
What do you know... who are you?
Kids...? No, how could you bring children to a dangerous place like this?!
Stay away! It's not safe here!
You're right. It's not safe.
You've inhaled a lot of smoke. Continue to do so, and you'll begin experiencing symptoms of smoke inhalation.
So what? Got anything useful to say?
I've never seen you before... you're Sankta, right? From Laterano? That's a holy statue of Laterano! Do something!
My mission does not involve the statue.
What are you talking about? Now's no time to talk about some 'mission'!
Use your... your... your Arts to put that bloody fire out, or get out of the way if you're not gonna help!
I'm afraid I'm unable to use Arts suitable for that purpose.
More people are needed to fight the fire and minimize damage.
I'll get help!
There's no need.
They're coming.
You're here...
We got word from Raimund.
Enough talk. Take the bucket! I'll go get more!
A-Alright!
More here!
I've got it.
Find a safe place to wait.
You're that Lateran...
And what of the kids?
I do not believe now is the right time to discuss that.
Hmph.
Here, bucket.
Clément!
Stop, Clément! Don't go any further!
I advise against going into the scene of the fire right now.
Let me go! Let me go!
It is dangerous without proper protective equipment.
He's right!
Are you trying to get yourself killed?!
I have to—
I have to...
Sorry... I lost my head.
I know it hurts, but this is serious.
Flowers can be replanted.
We're running out of water. I'll fill a few more buckets.
We can't go any further! The fire and smoke are too much...
You can stay behind.
I suggest not speaking loudly to avoid smoke inhalation.
And who's the reason I have to speak?
You don't need to follow me.
I don't trust you. I'm keeping an eye on you.
Wait, did you hear that?
The fire continues to burn, and the gun-wielding statue creaks ominously.
It casts a scorching shadow on Raimund, as though passing judgment on the Sarkaz who dared to set foot in a Sankta sacrarium, as it begins to topple in the fire.
Get back!
There is a sharp cracking sound, but the expected pain does not come.
The Executor stands before the Sarkaz, his gun held high.
This way, before anything else falls!
Your arm...
No, wait a second, you destroyed the statue?!
I did not destroy it, only changed its direction to prevent it from hitting you.
But it's your statue...
That does not matter.
The fire continues to grow. It's atypical.
Although furnishings like wooden benches are flammable, the initial location of the fire was unusual. It's possible that something else caused the fire.
What do you mean? What are you trying to say?
Hm?
That is...
(Is this where the Sarkaz live?)
(The walls are too thin to guard against the winds blowing across the wastes.)
(It really is chilly.)
(It's getting dark. I'm taking too long.)
(Hm?)
(Is that a fire I see up there?)
(A fire, now?)
(Looks like there's trouble on Federico's side.)
(...! Someone's coming!)
They've gone to help at the abbey. Should we go too?
Stefano was good to us. Even if we're leaving...
You go if you want to, but keep me out of this!
They've got their Lateran friends! Let them help. We're not welcome there.
(Well, guess I just got into the habit of hiding myself.)
(Was there an argument? Things weren't like this when Oren and I first came...)
Don't be mad. Leyla didn't mean to say that...
She definitely thought it, even if she didn't mean to say it!
She said I stole her winter clothes! Me, a thief? Taking care of this stuff has been my responsibility for years!
Man...
She thinks we're an eyesore! They all think so! They never treated us the same as them!
They hate us because Stefano refused to abandon us and return to Laterano! I see them for who they are now. We're the only ones who thought of us as family!
I hesitated when Gerald wanted to leave. Now, I realize he was right.
They want us gone? We'll be gone!
Can we really get out of here, though? The scouts haven't come back...
Still better than waiting. Next time this happens, I won't be able to stop myself from punching someone.
It's cold.
I spent so much time holed up in a room that I forgot how cold the night outside was.
Velliv is going to cut my pay for this.
I guess I'll make my own shining fruit dobosh for Mostima rather than buy it if that happens.
Time is running out...
—Found you!
It's finally out...
Everything is gone, but at least the fire didn't spread below. It could've been worse.
What do you mean, 'it could've been worse'?!
Look at the statue! And the flowers... oh.
No one was hurt. That's a good thing.
What about you, Raimund?
Fine. Not dead yet.
You crazy kid, you really charged straight in!
Just you wait, Gerald will smack you around when he hears about this!
Don't tell Gerald.
Ha, now you're scared.
Where's the Abbot? Did anyone tell him?
Well, informing him is about all we can do right now.
Everybody be careful. We don't want any more accidents...
It wasn't an accident.
Federico!
What did you say?
It wasn't an accident. I believe this was an act of arson.
That's not something to say lightly, Lateran.
Impossible... who would want to burn the sacrarium down?
Do you have proof?
Like I said.
Huh?
The fire spread in an unusual way. It was caused by a foreign source.
That's all?
Enough to reach a conclusion.
Why you—
Wait a second, Raimund.
What? You think this is arson too?
I... I don't know. But...
I went to look for help. People don't hang around this place much, especially you Sarkaz...
But I bumped into you.
Hey, what are you getting at?!
Don't interrupt, Fernand!
Let him finish.
I... I don't mean... I just wanted to ask...
What were you doing there, Raimund?
Is this what you wanted to show me, Gerald?
People don't say what's really on their minds around us.
This is our reality, Stefano.
I know. I've always known.
No one is wrong. This isn't anyone's fault. We just want to live in peace. But this is how things are.
The Sarkaz are doomed to wander.
I told you this place would be your home.
You did.
You said you wanted the children to take root here.
Maybe I was wrong.
You said no one is wrong.
There's a lot on your mind lately, Stefano.
What are you planning?
Or, should I ask...? Did you have anything to do with this fire?
What do you mean?
You're accusing me?
That's not what I meant...
What about you? Are you going to accuse me too?
Come now, Raimund, no one's making any accusations...
I didn't start any fires.
Then why are you here at this hour?
You don't pray with us. You don't come to the sacrarium. You... you people don't have any faith!
We may not have faith. But that doesn't mean we have anything to do with the fire.
I had some personal business that took me past this place. Believe me or don't.
What sort of business?
Nothing to do with you.
Hey...!
Enough. Is there some rule against us being here? You just want to accuse us of starting the fire, don't you?
We shouldn't have helped them fight the fire. We should have let the place burn to the ground.
What did you say?!
Fires have happened before, at this time of year. It could just be a coincidence.
What a coincidence it would be! You there, didn't you say someone started the fire? Come here...
Yes, I believe this was an intentional act.
And you think I did it?
No, I don't.
You're lying.
I have no reason to lie.
The fire spread quickly. It can be inferred that the arsonist was near the sacrarium.
And Raimund was—
Do not jump to conclusions. What I meant was that anyone who was near the scene is a suspect.
That includes all of you, and myself.
Wh-What are you talking about?
You think we started the fire? We're Sankta. Why would we burn our own sacrarium?
Race is not proof of innocence. I would not hesitate to destroy the sacrarium, if I thought it necessary.
I did not find any evidence implicating this Sarkaz.
Hmph, at least you're talking sense.
Senior Federico is right... we can't accuse anybody without evidence.
You're gonna just let this go?! It's the sacrarium! And all those flowers that you so painstakingly grew...
Please, that's enough!
I don't want to suspect any of us.
Clément...
The arguing must stop.
The possibility of the Sankta burning their own sacrarium cannot be eliminated. I will continue to investigate until I discover the true culprit.
Hey, wing-man, about what just happened...
Yes?
You're the one who started all that, alright? You're the one who first said that someone started the fire!
And you don't fully trust me, do you?
Yes.
Can you give me a little more than that?
Ahem. Anyway, what I mean is...
...Don't expect me to thank you.
I don't.
(He doesn't know how to say gratias, Sara.)
(Poor thing...)
(We can teach him! It's not hard.)
(But I'm a little scared of him...)
Who are you saying doesn't know how to say gratias?!
Hey, wing-man! Where did these kids come from? I've never seen them before!
Eek!
You don't know them?
Should I?
Very good. I want to ask you about the Sarkaz population here, and about these two children.
What? You...
I'll answer your questions.
Gerald! The fire...
I saw it all. You did well, Raimund.
And... we meet again, Reverend Executor.
I don't understand.
You were standing there for a long time. I thought you didn't intend to show your face.
Guess there's no hiding from you.
Why did you change your mind?
I believed you did not want to see me again.
Peace makes one timid.
Would you like to pay a visit to our place, Executor?
We don't have much, but there's a place to sit down, and you can see with your own eyes how the Sarkaz live here.
As for your questions... I'll do my best to answer them.
Phew...
I haven't been so active in a long time. I've never been good at pursuit.
However...
I've got you!
(Bizarre cry)
Quiet. This is a residential area. You'll bother the others.
(Growls)
Eat... hungry...
Oh, you can talk? This will be much easier if we can communicate.
(Strange roar)
I guess it's not that simple.
I'm sorry, but you'll have to stay put. It's dangerous.
I'll contact the Iberian Inquisition. I'm sure they know more about you than I would. If you're really sapient, maybe you can talk to their priests...
Whoa!
Who's that?!
Stop right there!
I must ask you to stay.
The night is cut open by a pulse of light.
Lemuen feels a heavy weight that forces her gun down.
The shadow that wields the light stands silently, drawing back the exotic sword before the gun that is slowly pointing towards him.
As the clouds disperse to reveal the moon, Lemuen begins to see who it is.
Well, there have been a lot of unwelcome visitors today.
Can I ask you to stop bothering me? I will be very, very angry if someone gets hurt because we wasted too much time here.
I'm afraid I cannot give way.
I must ask you not to stop that pitiful soul.
Hm?
What is it?
A noise. Source unclear.
You don't have a lot of people.
More than you think. Some went out and haven't returned.
It's getting late. Go back and have some rest.
Are you sure we're leaving tomorrow? Some people haven't come back yet.
We follow the plan as it is. We set out tomorrow morning.
Alright.
Senior Federico.
What is it?
I'm sleepy...
I'm sleepy, and hungry too!
Are you gonna take us home?
I want to go home... I want Mamma...
Have gone all day without food?
Yeah...
Mamma never eats. She says she's not hungry.
But we haven't eaten today. My tummy hurts.
It's so weird.
It's not weird. Food is one of the body's basic needs, and being deprived of it leads to discomfort and pain.
Mmm...
That's so complicated!
Is it normal to feel pain if you don't eat?
Has Mamma been hurting when she doesn't eat?
If what you say is true, yes, your mother has been suffering physical torment.
Enough, Reverend Executor!
This is a fact.
Waaaaaahhh...
Uwaaaahh!
Sometimes, the truth is better left unsaid.
*sniffles*...
Is Mamma hurting now?
I cannot confirm your mother's current status.
The Executor kneels down. No one pays attention to the dirt on the edge of his robe, and the adornments that represent his status.
Federico Giallo gazes straight into the children's eyes.
I am here as an executor of the Notarial Hall to investigate the truth of what is happening here at the monastery, and to maintain its peace and order.
The disappearance of an inhabitant is within the scope of my mission.
Do you mean...?
You'll help us find Mamma?
Yes, I will find your mother.
Really?
Yay!
I never would've thought...
Will you leave these children in my care for now, Reverend Executor?
I cannot make the decision for them.
You're right.
Will you go with this young man over here, children?
Me?
But I have to... well, I guess I can do it, now that the sacrarium's all burned up.
(It's the one even scarier than Senior Federico.)
(Yeah, he's so scary...)
You two...
That will do, Raimund.
He'll take you kids to supper, then it's off to bed, okay?
But I want to stay with Senior Federico...
Sara, Federico's busy! We need to be good.
O-Okay, we'll go with the scary guy...
Hahaha!
Boss!
A-Ahem.
Can you get the kids something to eat, then put them to bed?
I can... but where do I take them?
Hyman's house.
Hyman's? I guess there's nobody there right now... but what if Hyman comes back tonight?
It'll be fine.
Alright, then.
See you tomorrow, Senior Federico!
I hope we find Mamma tomorrow...
I will come for you in the morning.
Yay! Good night, Senior Federico.
Senior Federico, Senior Federico...
Yes?
I-I can't reach you!
Come down a little lower!
What?
Hehe...
G-Good night!
Hmph.
Quite the lady's man, aren't you?
Kids these days... what's so great about that guy?
Hey, wait up, you two! Don't run!
...Good night.
I thought this was going to be an easy trip, almost like a vacation, welcoming some brethren back to Laterano. It didn't sound hard at all, did it?
Well, I guess I did have to deal with Oren.
But that wasn't too much of a problem. It's not like you can't talk to him.
Certainly, your skills with a gun can solve many problems.
Not quite enough to deal with you, though.
You flatter me.
I'm not flattering you... should I call you Father?
You came to protect that... curious visitor of ours, didn't you? I suppose you're from Iberia?
I am.
You wear a cleric's robe, but you feel completely different to believers of the Lateran faith.
You're sharp.
Honest, aren't you?
I won't beat around the bush, then. I don't normally like to be so direct, but...
Not one more step forward. Leave, now.
You are not fit to set foot in Paradise.
I'm afraid I can't comply.
I am needed here, for a little longer.
(Tough customer.)
(An unusual sword. The style is Iberian...)
Your attacks are even sharper than your words.
I still harbor hope for mutual understanding, but I concede that you're acting appropriately, given your position.
I'm curious, though... how do you define Paradise?
A place where everyone lives in peace and happiness.
Preferably with less unwelcome visitors.
Would you call this place Paradise, then?
You're enjoying this chit-chat, aren't you?
A group of people have found peace away from the world for sixty years. What do you call this, if not Paradise?
Even if their lives are endless cold, hunger and suffering?
They may be wanting for material comforts... but it's far from endless.
Ah, yes. Laterano will bring salvation. The hard days will soon come to an end.
At the price of abandoning some.
...This isn't anyone's fault.
Please, don't get me wrong. I'm not accusing anyone.
But I've been thinking: What is the difference between us? Is it physical, or something else?
From the individual's very birth, all that makes us who we are causes our thinking to go in divergent ways and reach divergent conclusions...
Is there really such a thing as mutual understanding within this?
Are you really going to ask this to a Sankta?
You could try it yourself in your next life, if you happen to become a Sankta.
...*cough*!
(This has taken too long. Can't let that thing attack the residents.)
(I need to take care of this quickly...)
No need to be anxious. I don't really plan on doing anything.
I'm here because my kin need help.

You sound sincere, but I'm not in a position to trust you.
*cough* *cough*...
*sigh*
You're as stubborn as a former student of mine. It can be a virtue, but it can also be trouble.
I'm curious about a student who could cause you trouble.
One of the best I've ever had. We never saw each other again, after he left Iberia.
I didn't want to do this... I'm so getting in trouble for it.
Negotiations failed.
Can't say I regret it.
Lemuen movement is as light as her voice.
She pushes away the movement-aiding device beneath her. The lamp that hung on the handle sways in the night wind, falls, then shatters.
Light leaks out haphazardly, splashing on the silhouette slowly standing up in front.
The dark veil of night is lifted.
You can stand?
Not yet, really.
Rehabilitation is going to get longer, and I'm going to get scolded.
*hiss*

I trust you won't push yourself too hard.
You're not in good physical shape, nor are you used to this kind of battle—
Oh!
Thanks for the compliment. That was aimed for your mouth.
The people tune out the sermon, when it gets too long.
Especially if they're already fuming after a long and not very good day.
I think I can justify my response based on the provocation.
The cardinal aide narrows her eyes. The facade of meekness that built up from years of being bedridden falls apart in the sound of her gun being loaded.
Lemuen's light laugh has no warmth in it.
I'm tired. Let's do this quick.
I'll say it again—
—Get out of my way, Father.
You may not set foot in Paradise.
Hungry... so hungry...
Eat...
The thin door plank is pushed open from the outside.
The children are still sleeping.
The cold wind pours into the tiny, battered house through the gap. The unknown threat sneaks in soundlessly, and slowly approaches...
Eat...
Hungry... eat, eat...
Oh... oh...

lines.st: I rarely prayed when I was in Laterano.
This is not to say that my faith was flawed then, or that I was more arrogant or lazier than I am now. Nor can I prove that I am more devout, humble or diligent now.
Because of the environment, though, there were many things that I thought were not necessary when I was in Laterano.
In Laterano, all that the people sought was right there. The Holy City's blessing was right there.
It was everywhere.
The Sankta knew it.

lines.x: 'Lord, I seek your forgiveness...'
'I swore to welcome all who asked for help, treat all of them with equal kindness, and help them to the best of my ability.'
'But I did not keep my word.'
'I can no longer answer the calls for help. I cannot ask them to stay, for we are short on supplies and cannot afford it.'
'I must choose between one group and another.'
'Choose, yes, I have made many choices.'
'But choosing is wrong. And I cannot bear any more wrongs.'
'I cannot go back on my faith, abandon one to save another, save the many and condemn the few.'
'Laterano... my absent home...'
'Why is Laterano the only home?'
'If betraying the high guidance is the path to what I believe in; if only by accepting the heathen can obstacles be truly cleared...'
'...All will be revealed soon.'
'My Lord, forgive my sins... if you truly exist.'
Who's there?!
Come, now, there's no need for violence.
I've been looking for you, Most Reverend Abbot.
Food is in short supply these days. I hope you don't mind.
Not that minding would do any good. At least it fills the stomach, even if the taste is nothing to write home about.
I don't need to eat right now.
Eat. Don't let it go to waste.
Gerald, leader of Sarkaz. You knew the two Liberi children.
Are you always so direct?
Don't call me a leader. I've seen the true King of Sarkaz. A humble hunter is not worthy of the title.
Why do you call yourself a hunter?
Because that's what I am. I hunt to feed myself and others.
I think you have an idea of what's going on here, even though you just arrived today.
Not the full picture.
You lead the group of Sarkaz that lives here. You were latecomers, but at one point you got along well with each other.
Yes. At one point.
Now, conflict has arisen, and it is likely to escalate, given the circumstances.
Yeah, which is why we're leaving.
Normally, you would have the advantage in conflict with the other inhabitants.
There are few able combatants amongst them, which is one of the reasons they took you in.
But Laterano's intervention has taken away your martial advantage. Leaving is the logical choice.
You're good with those logical conclusions.
But I learned a long time ago—
Our lives are not logical—and I get reminders about that all the time.
The candlelight flickers.
The aging Sarkaz casts his gaze on the wall. The shadows reflected upon it have become blurred, one fading into another.
We didn't choose to leave because we couldn't live anymore.
Before I came here, I never thought we would have a life like this.
There were dangers, there was conflict, but we were accepted, we built our own homes, worked for our daily bread...
That all might seem normal to you, but it was beyond our wildest dreams.
What is the true reason you chose to leave?
It's simple.
We see the writing on the wall, just like you do. The rift can no longer be repaired. All that's left is to watch things get worse.
Today it's just an argument, an accusation, but what about tomorrow?
Violent confrontation between the natives and the Sarkaz is likely.
Today's fire was a warning.
Leave now, and we remain friends to be missed.
But if we allow ourselves to be burdened by attachment and fail to make a decision...

We will become the Sarkaz that the rest of the world knows—a band of vagabonds who bring conflict with them wherever they go, who are not welcome anywhere, who have no place to call home, and no place to go.
That's just prejudice.
To us, it's a fact.
The Sankta have Laterano.
But where do the Sarkaz belong to?
It's time.
It may take a while for the little ones to get used to life without a roof over their heads, but it'll happen.
As for those two children...
You knew them.
...I know a little about where they came from.
The people at the abbey and Raimund don't.
Very few do.
I guess they're wanderers who came from the outside. Their family could be dead, or they could have abandoned their children.
Stefano has taken in other wanderers before, but this place could hardly accommodate any more inhabitants these days.
But someone secretly took the children in.
You know who it was.
Few secrets can truly be kept forever, especially now, when every single grain counts.
Hyman took them in. She set aside a part of her own rations each day, and stole from the hunted game a few times. I covered it up for her when I discovered it.
Even then, life isn't easy for them.
Where is she now?
That's what I'm wondering. She should have come back with game today, but I haven't heard from her.
She's missing. We don't know where she is.
Whatever happened, I hope she doesn't do anything stupid.
Missing? No, this isn't right.
What do you mean?
Hyman would not allow the children to go out, in order to keep their existence a secret. Erendel and Estara's appearance at the sacrarium was not an accident.
Someone brought them outside.
Where is Hyman's home? I want to see it for myself.
The battered wooden hut was a shelter in the storm for the young children.
Abandoned by adults who lacked the means to raised them, they wandered the wastelands and were picked up by a Sarkaz on death's door.
The battered hut was leaky and cold, but there were clean clothes, even if a little tattered, and food, even if the taste was not great.
The walls did a poor job of keeping the cold out. They could gather all the fabric in the house and wear the thickest winter clothes they had, and they would still shiver at night.
But it was home. Mamma was there.
That was where the children felt safe.
The unexpected visitors do not disturb their soft breathing.
They remain slow and steady.
A twisted shadow is cast over the children.
(Incomprehensible whisper))
Hungry... eat...
Mm...
(Rolls over)
Eat, eat...
Cold...
The misshapen monster murmurs incomprehensibly.
The stationary shadow begins to move.
In the moonlight, the monster's powerful tail reaches forward. It brushes the children's soft cheeks, leaving behind a wet scent, then—
It falls into the soft, meek little 'prison.'
Still sleeping soundly, Estara and Erendel instinctively lean towards the comforting scent.
Cold...
Zzz... zzz...
Mamma...
Oh... hungry... eat, eat... cold...
Cover... cover...
Cold... cover...
Eren... Sara...
What have you done, Your Excellency?
It is improper to eavesdrop on a confession, Reverend Executor.
Oh, don't get me wrong, I wasn't listening to your confession.
Of course, you also know that's not what I'm talking about. Nor am I asking who you sent to ring the bell, how you plan meals for the monastery...
I'm asking about the smell coming from the sacrarium up there.
You can tell by the smell...? Is the Griffin Arsenal still in business?
As far as I know, it still supplies practice equipment for various educational institutions.
Including firebombs.
...Only the Holy City endures.
I feel your pain. If only Federico learned subtlety and discretion, right?
Still, it's just a corner of a building. It's far from over. Everyone can return to Laterano safely. What do you think?
You're talking to the wrong man.
I taught everyone how to dismantle the abbey's old weapons and make use of them. Fuel is short, while the winter is long.
But if you think I instigated the fire... I won't argue.
Come now, don't talk like that. Why don't we sit down and have a chat? Find a solution that we can all accept?
Chat? Solution? Ha.
Is there a solution that doesn't involve turning my back on a human being? I already explained my convictions. Would you truly accept a compromise?
A group of refugees sought sanctuary at the monastery seven years ago during a great drought. I closed the doors to them.
They stumbled, almost crawling, back to the wastes. It wasn't the first time I saw a sight like that.
But I swore before the Lord that it would be the last.
Am I wrong in my convictions, Executor?
No, of course you're right. I understand your pain.
There are limits to Laterano's power.
The laws are imperfect.
You...
I think, and therefore, I doubt.
For all its imperfections, though, Laterano is good enough for me. I don't want to see her peace and harmony to be threatened by anyone or anything.
I'm sure you can understand that.
It would be easier for me to persuade my colleagues if we reach an agreement here, and if you refrain from causing any complications.
My mission would be easier if none of you lifted a finger.
Just because not everyone can go back to Laterano, doesn't mean that no one should.
Laterano is to you as this place is to me.
There is no need for any further talk between us.
I ask you to leave me to my confession.
Raimund? It's getting late. You should get some rest.
...Clément.
I just had something to do.
I'm really sorry about today...
Don't think too much about it. We're leaving anyway.
...Right.
When is that...?
Depends on Gerald. We'll be leaving tomorrow morning, no matter what.
Probably after Matins.
Matins...
Oh! Do you know where Fortuna and Delfina are?
I was going to... well, I was looking for them.
Did... Gerald not tell you?
What? What are you talking about?
Delfina... she's gone.
Good... bye...
Was that a word of farewell? Or am I mistaken?
You possess basic language skills. It seems my previous assessment was incorrect.
You... are...
Sank... ta.
Indicating a certain level of sapience and cognitive thinking.
Communication appears possible, despite deviation of physical form.
Unknown level of contagion. Unclear if movement patterns change over time. Unknown level of danger.
Notarial Hall regulations permit elimination of potential threats of unknown danger level in order to safeguard the rights of the citizenry.
No sign of resistance. No apparent will to survive.
I'll try to make this quick and painless.
The monster stands in place.
The path before it is blocked. It cannot go back to the past behind.
Justification, anger, pain, all consumed within the no-longer-human body, before it can manifest itself on the hardened hide.
Before an obstacle it cannot surmount, the monster loses the strength to fight, but cannot let go of its last bit of pride.
It stands, silent.
Like a statue.
Wait!
Executor, no!
Ge..rald...
Hyman... I had half a mind to start praying like Stefano, praying that the monster the Executor was talking about wasn't you.
It was the logical hypothesis. It has now been confirmed.
Move aside, Gerald.
No.
You're interfering with official business.
I know. You can point your gun at me, pull the trigger, bump off a Sarkaz criminal. You won't get any complaints from me.
But I can't let you kill Hyman.
...Explain.
I can't give you a 'correct' or 'proper' explanation.
All I can say is—
Look behind Hyman. There are two children sleeping beyond that door.
Children...
My, children...
They have no idea what happened. They're full of hope that you will find their mother.
They trust you.
The Sarkaz's rough, weathered hand presses against that of a Sankta.
There is no brotherly intimacy, no trust or understanding between friends.
But the Sarkaz's hand presses the Sankta's.
The muzzle of the gun drops, inch by inch.
Don't do it, Executor.
Don't do it, unless you've reached a conclusion that you're satisfied with.
There's no sign of fighting inside the house. The children are still sleeping.
Hyman wouldn't harm the kids. You can be sure of that.
Not yet.
I don't know how long she can maintain her reason.
Thank you.
How do you intend to tell the kids about Hyman when they wake up?
They have a right to know.
Sometimes, ignorance is bliss.
Is that a suggestion?

No, Federico...
I hope you don't mind me calling you that.
It was you who spared Hyman. It was you who earned the children's trust. The choice is yours.
Feeling the weight?
I'm not wearing any extra weights.
I'll take that as your personal brand of humor.
What are you looking at?
Do you know what this is?
A piece of cloth... we stuff like that to make blankets for winter, but I've never seen this pattern before.
It's a common Lateran pattern.
Underneath the cloth, the twins' breathing is long and steady.
The Executor looks at it for a while, then turns his gaze to the simple desk beside.
There is a small clay jar on it.
Other than the children, the flower in the jar, not yet fully wilted, provides the only color in the room.
A flower? It's pretty dried-up. Must have been there for at least two days.
Is this Hyman's hobby?
I... I don't know.
I thought I knew the people who follow me. But I'm not even sure...
I must return to the sacrarium.
I have enough information to pass judgment on the Sarkaz.
And what is your judgment?
First, there is something I must know—have you had any interaction with Oren Argiolas?
Oren the nuncio?
Yes.
Not much. He went missing soon after he arrived.
I see.
You call yourself Gerald, but that is not your real name.
Who can say if a name is real or not? I've used it for ten years.
I've been Gerald the Hunter ever since we started living here and built our first house with those broken planks.
How you identify is none of my concern. You can be Gerald.
Then—
A strange sense of urgency causes the words fall from the tongue.
Federico does not give him time to speak.
He says the name that has not been spoken in public for ten years.

A mercenary who made his name in the Kazdel Civil War, wanted by the Notarial Hall, thirty-two confirmed crimes on his record, responsible for annihilating three of the Holy Lateran's Executor Maniples.
Holst Tyfindale.
You knew...
Yes, I knew. What I can find out, others can too.
This is your final warning—
Your identity is no longer a secret, Gerald.
Target structure confirmed.
Hard to believe the Sarkaz would really want to live in an abbey of ours.
Ready to move in?
No rush. We'll wait for the order.
Let's wait and see. We have the area surrounded.
Same as that Sarkaz team we nabbed earlier...
Not a single one of them is getting away.
Yes, sir!
I got some food. It's not much, but it's better than nothing.
Sorry, Serpilia... I'm not hungry...
Well, I'll leave it here. You might start to feel hungry in a bit.
Gratias, Serpilia...
What for?
Nothing in particular... I just wanted to thank you.
Eat up and go to bed if you really want to thank me. I get off earlier that way.
R-Really? Then I'll go to sleep...
Sorry, I can't sleep...
I figured.
Are you sure you should be here? I'm feeling much better now... what I mean is... you don't have to stay with me if you don't want to.
Watching you is my job, girl. I'm not exactly a saint.
I won't run away.
Who knows? The better a girl behaves, the more you have to keep an eye on her.
Do you know what those black horns on your head mean? Do you know what's waiting for you when you're brought back to Laterano?
I... No, I don't.
Of course you don't. You can't empath with me, can you?
Oh, don't look like that. I was just trying to scare you a little. His Holiness is pretty kind, all things considered. No need to worry too much.
I'm not worried.
Whatever punishment I receive, I deserve...
What's that sound? Is there someone outside?
Whatever it is, it's none of your business.
Stay put. I'll check it out.
Huh?
Strange. There's nobody here.
You're really letting your guard down. It hasn't been that long.
The way you talk still makes me want to punch you.
I'm wondering how you operated in Victoria. Did you spend all your time making enemies for Laterano, Oren?
I resent that characterization. No one takes his job more seriously than me.
Speaking of job, you're ready on your side, aren't you, Serpilia?
Maybe?
I need a less ambiguous answer.
We move at dawn, if there are no complications.
(Strange... Serpilia hasn't come back yet?)
(Things seemed to have calmed down outside...)
(Wh-What's that sound?)
S-Serpilia? Is that you?
Oh! Mmph...
Shh.
It's me.
Raimund?!
Come with me, Fortuna.
Finally outside.
Why are you here, Lemuen?
There was a bit of trouble. I ran into someone unpleasant and let him get away... it sounds even worse when I put it that way.
I came because I heard a commotion over here.
I'm surprised you would make a threat like that.
Threat?
Wasn't it?
Then what did you mean?
I was only telling the truth. Correct judgment is based on the truth of the present situation. I don't need to threaten anyone.
I think I get it...
But there is nothing more unpredictable than people. Even we fail to understand our own thoughts sometimes, and it's not unusual for our actions and thoughts to contradict one another.
Experience tells me that things rarely develop in the most logical way.
Setting that aside... the situation is more complicated than I expected.
We need to exchange information, Federico.
You mean the monster we saw was the missing Sarkaz by the name of Hyman?
The state that you saw her in was different from what I saw. And if that's the case...
She has gone from being impossible to communicate with, demonstrating no capacity for thought beyond feeding instinct, to being able to communicate in a limited manner.
Evolution.
That is a reasonable hypothesis.
She has undergone a transformation. During a mission, I read about a religious cult that operates on the Iberian coastline.
The Church of the Deep.
You seem to know a lot about them, Federico. Have you seen similar creatures on previous missions?
Rarely.
But you have. That makes things easier to explain.
I had reason to read up on it a while ago... we may be working with the Iberian Inquisition concerning that cult.
They've caught His Holiness's attention. I didn't expect to run into them here...
It's a difficult problem.
Difficult and dangerous... I doubt I would've gotten the upper hand if that visitor was trying to do more than slow me down.
This is irritating... I really need to get back to form. Even Serpilia will start looking down on me at this rate.
Back to the situation at hand, are you sure you should have told Gerald all that?
He has a right to know.
Alright. What's the plan now?
Investigate.
What?
Fires don't start on their own. Something is not right with the fire at the sacrarium.
And the flower... No, I can't be sure yet.
First, I need to find what caused the fire.
Sorry to wake you at this hour.
It's okay. A lot of us couldn't sleep anyway.
I've let everyone know. We can leave at any time.
But didn't you say we were leaving in the morning? What changed?
Is it because of the fire?
I guarantee you it wasn't Raimund who did it. We don't need to—
No, I don't suspect him.
Then...
We have a situation. I'll explain later. But we can't wait until morning.
Still no word from the team we sent out?
No. It's strange. They might have run into trouble.
Should I take a few guys and check it out?
No, we'll go together. Make sure everyone sticks together. No one wanders off on their own.
I'll scout ahead. Have the old folks come with me. You take the rear guard with Raimund, alright?
Something's not alright. I can see it on your face.
What's going on? You're anxious. Why?
Because of the Laterans?
Nothing gets past you.
You're always cautious, Reed.
Stop. That's not my name anymore.
Sorry, I just...
Gerald cannot finish his sentence.
He realizes why his old friend could tell something was wrong at a glance. He realizes he had his hand on the hilt of his dagger.
He is turning from Gerald the Hunter to the mercenary he once was.
Sorry. It felt too much like our Kazdel days.
Maybe I'm overthinking it, but I have a bad feeling about this. Call it a hunch, but we should leave as soon as possible.
Yes, as soon as possible. Take the ones who are ready. We go now.
Like I said, you're the boss. Your call.
But the last time you had a hunch, it was about finding a place and having a better life.
We'll find it. I swear.
I believe you.
You found this place for us.
I hope it goes smoothly.
I'm sorry, Eileen, but I can't take you this time.
Will you blame me for it?
What would you think if you could see this?
Who's there?
It's me, Gerald.
Are you busy?
There's something you should know...
Wow, that old man is intimidating, isn't he?
This mission has gotten seriously complicated.
Sarkaz, a fallen angel, and a mad abbot... give me a break.
And I'm here with Federico, of all people... is that a good thing or not?
Just in case...
Oren, it's me.
Are you gonna do it? You've got the guys there, right?
I suggest you look into this Bishop Stefano. Something's not right with him. It could be dangerous.
A hunch?
Maybe... I have a bad feeling about this abbey. Well, I guess that's not the right way to put it... it's bad because things aren't that bad.
You've been here longer than I have. Don't you feel it?
How could there be a place where everyone works so hard, yet feels no hope at all?
That's such a sad thing.
So, I told Raimund about Delfina and Fortuna, not knowing that he didn't know.
He got very worked up. I'm concerned.
Gerald...? Are you okay?
I'm fine. Don't worry.
It's not your fault. I just didn't have the chance to tell him.
Where's Raimund?
He ran out after asking me about Fortuna. He said he was going to get her back.
They have Laterans guarding Fortuna. Are you sure about this?
The Sankta won't allow it.
Raimund was born here. Things are different out there. He doesn't understand our relationship with the Sankta.
I remember, you've said that relations between the Sankta and Sarkaz are...
Bad.
It might not have been so bad if we were all just civilians...
Won't Raimund be in danger?!
I'll find him. Thanks for telling me.
There isn't much I can do...
I'm beginning to think you were right, Gerald. You should leave this place.
Go now. It's the right choice. Don't... don't wait until tomorrow!
That's the plan.
You seem anxious, Clément... did something else happen?
I'm fine. I'm just... today has been quite a day. It's a little too much.
Go, Gerald. You were right. You shouldn't stay here.
Something's off about you, Clément. Calm down. You—
Boss! We have a problem!
What is it?
We went the opposite way from the scout team, as you had ordered, and we saw troops approaching the abbey!
Troops?
Laterans! Sankta and Pontifica Cohors!
I fought them many times. I'd know 'em from a mile away!
They're going to surround us!
They never intended to let us go!
Gerald draws his fingers back with a start.
A moment later, he realizes that it's because his fingertips are too cold, so cold that the leather sheath seems to be burning.
Clément.
I'm here. What do we do...?
Don't worry.
I've been running for too long. This is just putting things back the way they should be... all will be settled.
Tell us what to do, boss.
We're prepared to fight.
Thank you for spending all these years with me, all of you.
Don't talk like that.
What are you doing, Gerald? What are you doing?!
P-Put down that knife! Calm down!
I'm perfectly calm, Clément..
The Sarkaz exhales, clutches the dagger, and pulls it out from its sheath.
It burns.
For an instant there, he had difficulty holding onto his old friend.
My mind has not been so clear in a decade.
If only blood can end it all, then there's only one thing left to do.
Don't worry. The blood of Sarkaz is not shed lightly.
Raimund... wait, Raimund!
What are you doing?!
Taking you away from here.
I know what's going on. I won't let them take you!
No need to be scared. Come with me. We'll help you—
N-No! I'm not scared!
Raimund! Let me go... you don't get it!
Sorry... was I hurting you?
I'm okay.
I-I decided to go with them to Laterano, Raimund.
What?
What are you saying? Did they threaten you? You'll die if you go there!
I deserve to!
Let me go, Raimund. No one threatened me. I killed Fina. I can't run away from this!
Delfina...? That was an accident...
No! I don't want to hear that anymore, especially from you!
An accident... an accident! Of course I wouldn't point a gun at Fina on purpose. It was a tragic accident... but she's dead! Dead!
She'll never open her eyes again. She'll never speak to us again!
Fortuna, calm down... you'll hurt yourself!
I want to calm down... I don't want to be crying like this!
But why doesn't anyone blame me? Why does everyone call it an accident? As though that means Fina's death didn't matter?!
I killed Fina! What difference does it make that it was an accident?!
Breathe. Don't hold it in.
Huff... hah...
Huff...
Feeling better?
I'm sorry, Raimund. I shouldn't be like this, but I don't know what to do...
I only know that I can't go. I can't run away and pretend nothing happened. I can't accept that it was an accident.
This is my sin...
No more talking.
Don't worry about me, Raimund. It might not be that bad.
Serpilia, that Lateran emissary who came today, she was kind to me. I wasn't mistreated.
Maybe they'll lock me up when we get to Laterano.
I have horns now... I don't know if I'm Sankta anymore. It doesn't really matter if I'm not...
Anyway, I'm fine, Raimund. You—
No.
Huh?
I can't let it happen.
I can't just leave you like this, Fortuna—

Raimund? What are you...?
Eek!
(Hush! Hold your breath...)
(Someone's coming!)
He hung up.
When did you get Richele over to your side?
That's an exaggeration. He's always been that type who only does what's good for him.
He's even better than I am at looking out for himself...
I feel like I'm being watched...
Has your paranoia finally caused you to hallucinate, Oren?
I'd rather believe that there really is someone watching in the shadows.
Just a joke. Don't look at me like that.
Where's the fallen angel you were watching?
Sound asleep, I would think.
Stay on topic.
What were you saying about pushing the schedule forward? Not that you can't, but that's not what we agreed on.
Why the sudden change? Give me a reason.
What difference does it make to you?
Not much, which is why I can choose not to cooperate.
Unless my memory deceives me, I have command of the troops.
I'm prepared to write a report.
You're anxious, Oren. What's the matter?
This is a secret operation. It's not so easy to deploy troops, if the matter of the saints didn't catch His Holiness's attention.
Even then, His Holiness seems to have noticed something, and specifically sent Federico... you know how hard it is to pull wool over Federico's eyes.
Do you really think you succeeded?
Probably not.
But I would say I know him well enough. There won't be much trouble if he doesn't pursue the matter. Our goal is just to stop everyone who wants to leave, isn't it?
On the other hand, the beast is out of the bag the moment you give the order to attack.
Federico isn't concerned about the means, only the end.
Oh, you think so?
Let me just make it clear that if you get on his bad side, I can't stop him.
So much for camaraderie.
A message? Who could it be at this hour...? Oh.
Who is it?
See for yourself.

lines.st: Bring Oren to the narthex immediately.
—Federico

lines.x: So much for the wool.
*sigh*...
Everything you talked about, I'll take care of. Do as I say.
We take control of the abbey immediately. Get all available hands on it.
...That urgent?
An old man has given the last sixty years of his life to the abbey. He went so far as to imprison Lateran emissaries for the sake of those Sarkaz.
He suddenly became open to compromise in desperate circumstances, even proposing something so ritualistic as a final farewell... why do you think?
That, plus some Sarkaz who are not entirely unknown, and 'visitors' hiding in the shadows...
We have to act quickly.
I've got a bad feeling...
Take control? What does he mean... why?
Don't you understand? The Sankta lied to us!
I have to tell Gerald! I knew it! Those Laterans never intended to play nice.
The situation has changed. It's dangerous for you to be with me. Find a safe place; I doubt they'll do anything to you immediately—
Wait, Raimund!
*deep breath*
I changed my mind. I'm coming with you.
My, my, what a coincidence to run into you two!
I'm so glad you're safe, Lemuen.
Any news?
You look concerned, Executor Richele.
I found something that I thought warranted investigation. It has to do with the abbot, but... anyway, more investigation is needed.
What about you, Federico? What are you doing?
Work.
Um, ok, that much is obvious... what did you find? Something to do with the Sarkaz?
Yes.
I heard last night's fire was started by angry Sarkaz. Is that true?
No.
What's this? Rehearsing your stand-up comedy routine?
Enough jokes.
Jokes?
Oren and Serpilia will be here soon.
Well, by the sound of it, you knew Oren hid on purpose from the get-go?
Obviously.
That's just his luck, isn't it? He just can't seem to get away from you, whether at the Summit of Nations or now.
I allowed Oren to act of his own accord, because the complicated situation at the Sanctilaminium Ambrosii called for a Plan B.
But maybe he needs a reminder...
So, what's going on? I was told to come immediately and bring Oren.
Ah, there you are, Oren. Finally decided to show your face?
It's been a while, Lemuen.
Can't you be a little nicer to me? We're partners on this mission, after all.
It's because we're partners that you're still able to talk.
Whatever your plan was, the situation here doesn't call for that kind of aggression. It's time to stop, Oren.
Couldn't you two pretend you didn't see anything, like that guy over there?
Oh, er, I would prefer if you didn't drag me into this...
I will deal with Richele when we return to the Notarial Hall.
Gratias, Oren.
As for you, Oren.
If you truly believe there is a need for you to operate independently, you may try to persuade me.
Well, since there's no one else here, I'll cut straight to the chase.
Did you really think I wouldn't notice that you were trying to let those two kids go?
A fallen Sankta and a Sarkaz. Even a blind man would notice.
...Hmph.
You...
I've been patient for far too long.
Are you criticizing my methods?
Not at all, not at all.
But I have my own methods. We've wasted too much time here. It's we need to consider how to can get this done quickly, with no loose ends.
And so you decided to deploy a special forces unit?
You make it sound so easy. You know full well that deploying troops and giving the order are two different things.
Do you know how hard it was to come up with a justification that even His Holiness could not rebuke?
Now, I have one.
That Sarkaz boy—Raimund? Arson of a sacrarium is a serious offense, that's before I mention that he's Sarkaz. We're perfectly justified in seizing him and taking the investigation into our hands.
He was not the one who set fire to the sacrarium. Your justification is baseless.
I have no need of your intervention until I uncover the truth.
The truth? Ha. Is the truth really important?
What is more rational and logical than taking control and securing order?
Did you not consider it, Federico? Is the truth really so important to you?
Did you not consider the possibility of the locals resisting, Oren?
I didn't think you'd say something so silly, Lemuen. If the Sarkaz want to resist...
So much the better.
Who's there?!
The sound of knocking echoes through the hall.
The door creaks open, and the unusually bright rays of the moon pour in through the gap.
There is a figure standing in the moonlight.
Senior Federico...
Clément?
I finally found you, Senior Federico.
I was asked to bring you this...
By whom? What is—
The rest of the sentence vanishes into the night.
The tall and thin figure approaches, his back to the moonlight, his head bowed.
It is as if all the light in the room had converged upon him, but the overly bright light also casts a dark, suffocating shadow.
Now, Federico sees what Clément is holding.
What he said he was asked to bring to Federico.
A dagger.
Old, but no less sharp for it, once hidden in the belt of a certain someone.
Next to the dagger...
There is a package wrapped in cloth that has been dyed a deep shade of red, from which some sort of liquid continues to drip.
What is it?
It is...
The sinner, Holst Tyfindale, offers his head freely.
The sinner had admitted responsibility for all the crimes, all the evils committed hitherto.
He has now paid his price. All that remains is a band of insignificant vagrants.
Will the reverend emissaries of Laterano show grace and mercy?
Senior Federico... I bring you Gerald's last words.
He begs you take the head of the fugitive Holst and his weapon...
In exchange, spare Fortuna and Raimund...
Spare the other Sarkaz.
Look here, Holst!
You've got to hand it to Clément. He really planted the flowers in front of the statue, prettier than anywhere else.
I'm surprised Stefano was okay with it, digging a hole in the sacrarium floor, searching for the right dirt, all that.
Take it easy. You're injured.
It's just a little scratch.
Even little scratches can get worse.
And call me Gerald.
Alright, alright. You're starting to nag like Stefano.
That's why his face is all wrinkly. Don't end up like him.
Worry less, meddle less. That's the key to a long life.
Don't say that. Stefano wants the best for the abbey.
It's thanks to him that we get to live here.
I know, I'm just saying he worries too much.
I'm afraid it'll become too much for him.
Stefano is considered stubborn, even amongst the Sankta. He won't budge on what he thinks is right.
But that's one of the reasons we've found refuge here.
He says the Sarkaz are just like other people.
And he's right.
Well, you and I, we'll have his back if things become too much for him. We'll repay our debt.
And stop thinking about what happened in the past. We turned our backs on Theresa when we left Kazdel. We can't go back, Gerald.
Neither of us can.
...I know.
You don't need to remind me.
I don't remember when I stopped believing in Her Highness's vision... I couldn't see the future of the Sarkaz in our two rulers.
It's okay.
I'm fine. We have a new life now.
I led those who wanted to leave away from Kazdel. I'll take responsibility.
Life is much better than it used to be.
Be careful. We don't have the same resources we did before, and there are some tough customers among those bandits.
Aren't you being a little too careful?
How long will it take for the infamous mercenary to become a humble hunter out in the middle of nowhere? Five? Ten? Hahaha!
Haha...
...Gerald.
Yes?
Have you really thought about what you'll look like when you put down your weapons for real, and settle in as a humble hunter?
What will I look like?
No, I've never thought about it.
What will I look like...?
You'll see for yourself, Eileen.
A long time ago, Gerald used to look up at the night sky when he needed a breather after killing.
The sky is an oppressive black. The campfire has gone out, and even the embers have cooled.
Bloodied mercenaries wipe away the dirt from their faces, gazing at their equally battered comrades, briefly pondering questions they hardly have the luxury to think about.
Can Sarkaz like them truly hope for a happy life?
He once believed that the day would come when the Sarkaz rebuilt their home, but he feels more helpless with each passing day.
They have shed too much blood. They will run out of blood before their monarch's vision comes true.
Eventually, Gerald abandoned Kazdel, bringing along those gasping for breath, running from the land they once fought to the death for.
They hid from the world, ultimately finding a place that would take them. All they wanted was a normal, peaceful life.
The dream came true, once.
When did life turn bitter and oppressive?
It is like a fine dish on the table that no one has tasted.
It has gone bad through no one's doing.
If only blood can end this all...
Then there is only one thing left to do.
Don't bother trying to change my mind, Clément.
I can't go, and I won't go.
I never told you my old name. It's not a past I'm proud of. An infamous deserter, a broken man.
With me here, they have an excuse to harm the others.
Don't be so sad. I'm just going to see Eileen. Death is familiar enough to me, I used to trade in death.
My life is a small price to pay for the lives of all the others.
And don't worry about the ones left behind. They'll follow my order.
Raimund is young. He might be impetuous. Do try to calm him down, won't you?
Finally...
Tell Stefano that we probably shouldn't have stopped, back when we were wandering the wastes. Maybe we shouldn't have tried to stay.
But this place was too good.
It was like a dream, living together for so many years. Sooner or later, though, we all have to wake up.
Tell Stefano... gratias, and me paenitet.
I'll leave the rest to you, Clément.
Here, at the end of it all...
Am I a hunter, or am I still that mercenary?
I'm sorry, Eileen. I don't suppose you'd like what I've become, what I look like now.
...Dawn has broken.
It seemed like a lot has happened, but then it also seemed like it all happened in the blink of an eye.
It was a long night, wasn't it?
What are you doing?
Nothing worthy of your time, Senior Federico.
I just wanted to find a flower. Gerald shouldn't go to see Eileen empty-handed.
But it looks like the fire last night burned everything. There's nothing left.
If it's just a flower you want, you wouldn't need to come here.
You don't understand, Senior Federico. It's not the same.
The flowers here were different. A special breed grown only in this monastery.
They represent friendship and hope.
Yes, friendship and hope. The abbot told me they were a symbol of Laterano's partnership with Iberia, one of the abbey's most important possessions.
Beautiful. I was struck, immediately.
There used to be flowerbeds all over the abbey, covering every inch when you looked down from the bell tower during bloom season.
Eileen loved the sight of it. So did Gerald.
Many were scared to talk to the Sarkaz. But they got along a lot better after the first bloom season.
People realized that the cruel and warlike Sarkaz would stop to take a look when they passed by the flowerbeds. They would take a flower or two to put in their homes.
They weren't fearsome desperados. They were no different from us.
There is one here.
Ah, you mean the one beneath the statue.
I saw it. It was lucky to lie beneath the statue when it fell.
But it's not the one I wanted.
Why?
Look at it. Don't you see?
It may have hid itself beneath the statue, but its stem is broken, and its petals are burned.
But it's still here.
Is it?
I used to believe there were things that would survive, no matter the adversity. You may laugh, but I truly did.
Life wasn't easy, but all was good.
Amita Nina would make snacks for the kids when she has the time.
Have you ever heard of herb pizzelle?
Amita Nina and Hyman made them from a Lateran recipe using Sarkaz herbs. A dessert that everyone loved, one that belonged to this abbey, and this abbey alone.
Amita Nina used to say that tasting bitter fruit is all well and good, but the children should grow up knowing more than bitterness.
They should know what sweetness tastes like.
I will commit this dessert to memory.
Ha. It's not important. We forgot the taste of sweetness a long time ago.
No one makes pizzelle anymore.
I find myself curious at times.
If Sankta weren't so special, if everyone in Laterano was mundane, normal, insignificant as I am...
Would the Holy City still look the same?
Would Laterano be Laterano?
...I don't know.
That's a shame.
Clément...
Do you no longer have faith? Have your beliefs changed?
I was just made to see reality.
Things are not as immutable as people think. Testing them only harms them.
One little flaw, one little imperfection, one tiny, little wound, and it's broken beyond repair.
Like the flower you found.
The flower I'm looking for is no longer here.
